articles.title: The World's Weirdest Natural Disasters
articles.author: Marcia Amidon Lusted
articles.description: Strange and surprising natural disasters have happened throughout history.
articles.intro: Natural disasters like earthquakes, tsunamis, and hurricanes, happen all over the world and cause huge amounts of damage. In this article, Marcia Amidon Lusted tells about rare and surprising natural disasters from history.
articles.annotationTask: As you read, take notes on why these natural disasters are “weird.”
articles.permissions: The World's Weirdest Natural Disasters By Marcia Amidon Lusted. Copyright © 2022 by CommonLit, Inc. This text is licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

blocks.text: In West Africa, a lake spits out a cloud and, hours later, over a thousand people are dead. A fireball lands in Russia, with the force of 185 atomic bombs. And a volcanic explosion in one part of the world creates a year’s worth of terrible weather around the globe. Weird, right? Most of us think of natural disasters as things like hurricanes, tornadoes, earthquakes, and floods. But these aren’t the only kinds of disasters that people have had to deal with in the past. And while any kind of natural disaster can cause destruction, injuries, and deaths, history tells us there is a collection of natural disasters that can only be classified as “weird.”
Eighteen Hundred and Froze to Death

Imagine an entire year with temperatures so low that few crops can grow, and there is snow or frost every single month. Well that happened! The year was 1815, and in North America it became known as “the year without a summer,” or the year “Eighteen Hundred and Froze to Death.” Rivers and lakes were covered with ice in July and August. Eighteen inches of snow fell in New England in July. Birds froze to death and fell out of the sky. There were food shortages. People couldn’t find enough firewood to heat their homes.

What could have caused such terrible weather? It was something that happened on the other side of the world, on the island of Sumbawa in Indonesia. On April 10, 1815, Mount Tambora exploded in the biggest volcanic eruption that the world has ever seen. It spewed ash, stone, and volcanic rock — enough to cover a 200-square-mile area, twelve feet deep! It killed 12,000 people. But the effects of the Tambora explosion went far beyond Indonesia. A huge amount of ash, dust, and various gasses were sent 10 miles into the air. It created a cloud so dense that it reflected sunlight away from the earth and cooled its atmosphere by a half degree. Temperatures fell all over the world, and people in Europe, North America, and Asia were starved. It took three years for the effects of the 1815 volcano to completely disappear and for temperatures to return to normal.
The Tunguska Event

It was June in the year 1908, just past 7 a.m., and a man was sitting on his porch in Vanavara, Siberia, a remote area in Russia. Suddenly, he was thrown from his chair, and a wave of heat rolled over him, hot enough that he thought his shirt was on fire. There was a deafening bang, and a fiery ball streaked across the sky. Forty miles from where the man had been sitting, an asteroid had entered earth’s atmosphere at a speed of 33,500 miles per hour. It became so hot that it exploded 28,000 feet above the earth in a remote place called Tunguska.

The explosion, which scientist call a meteor air burst, was as powerful as 185 atomic bombs and ripped apart eight hundred square miles of forest. Eighty million trees were instantly flattened in a circular pattern. People and animals close to the site were either injured or died. Even one hundred miles away from the impact, windows shattered in houses. Sensors as far away as England recorded the shockwaves. Scientists believe it is the largest asteroid to hit earth in modern times.
The Lake Nyos Disaster in Cameroon

Lake Nyos was a serene, blue lake in the West African country of Cameroon. The people who lived near the lake were farmers who raised cattle and grew food for their families. But on the peaceful evening of August 21, 1986, Lake Nyos became deadly. Farmers living nearby heard a rumbling sound, and suddenly a frothy plume of spray shot out of the water hundreds of feet into the air. A white cloud formed above the lake and began drifting across the water and onto the land.

Curious farmers came out of their homes to see what had happened. Many of them immediately fell unconscious. The cloud continued drifting, and soon covered a deep valley. The people there, those both inside and outside their homes, instantly collapsed and died. Cattle also died in the fields. It continued like this, as the cloud kept moving, people and animals, as far as fifteen miles away from the lake, collapsed and died. On August 23, the cloud finally lifted. It left behind only a few survivors, people who would wake at various times to find themselves surrounded by dead bodies. A total of 1,800 people were killed.

Why was the Lake Nyos cloud so deadly? Scientists discovered that it was made of carbon dioxide (CO2) mixed with air. When there is too much CO2, it replaces the oxygen in the body’s cells. This causes suffocation, unconsciousness, and can lead to death. As the cloud kept moving, the amount of CO2 increased, killing people instantly. Researchers finally found a layer of CO2 on the bottom of the lake, but they have been unable to discover where it came from. A bubble of this CO2 was released when a rockslide took place on the shore. This forced the lake to burp up the CO2, which sprayed into the air and created the deadly cloud. Today Lake Nyos is no longer blue. It is now shallower and has turned a rusty color because of dark brown water that came up from the lake’s bottom with the spray. Scientists have installed huge plastic pipes in the lake that constantly bring water from the bottom to the surface. This lets the CO2 slowly bubble out and dissolve, so it won’t burp out again and cause another tragedy in the future.

options.label: Many natural disasters in the world are still mysteries to scientists.

articles.title: All Rise for Ketanji Brown Jackson!
articles.author: India James
articles.description: Students will read about the life and career path of the first Black female United States Supreme Court Justice, Ketanji Brown Jackson.
articles.intro: The U.S. Supreme Court has the final ruling over any court in the country. It is in Washington, D.C. The decisions that Supreme Court Justices make affect many lives. This article explains how Ketanji Brown Jackson became the first Black woman to serve as a judge on the Supreme Court.
articles.annotationTask: As you read, take notes on the steps Ketanji Brown Jackson took to become a Supreme Court justice.
articles.permissions: "All Rise for Ketanji Brown Jackson!" by India James. Copyright © 2023 by CommonLit, Inc. This text is licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

blocks.text: The clocks struck noon on June 30th, 2022. History was about to be made. People in the U.S., and many others around the world, were glued to their TV sets, their computers and mobile devices as they watched and waited. Ketanji Brown Jackson laid her left hand on a smooth, black Bible. She raised her right hand as she repeated the Judicial Oath. And with that, in front of her friends and family, Ketanji became the first Black, woman justice of the U.S. Supreme Court.
Early years
It was like a dream come true. Ketanji, whose name means “lovely one,” had always wanted to become a lawyer and even a judge. And now she is one of the most important judges in the country!
She was born on September 14, 1970. Both of her parents were teachers. Though Brown Jackson was born in Washington, D.C., she and her family soon moved to Florida.
She joined her school’s speech and debate team. She practiced using her words to persuade people. Her debate coach helped her to see that she could reach for her dreams.
“It really was the high school debate coach who took an interest in me and encouraged me,” Brown Jackson said.
She was also her class president and voted “most likely to succeed.” Everyone knew she had a bright future.
College, law school, and being a lawyer

Brown Jackson went to Harvard University after high school. She studied how the U.S. government works. She thought that judges weren’t always fair and wanted to understand how the courts could make better judgements.

Getting a law degree was important, but it wasn’t the only part of Brown Jackson’s education. She worked as a clerk, a person in charge of the records and reports, for three different judges after she finished law school. Part of a clerk’s job is to help judges do research for their cases and write their decisions. She learned all she could while working for these judges.
“Be open to new ideas and experiences,” said Brown. “You’ll never know when someone else will have an interesting thought or when a new door will open to take you on the journey of your dreams.”

After graduation, Brown Jackson worked as a lawyer. Sometimes she worked for the government. Sometimes she worked for different businesses. She was always concerned about making trials and judgements fair.
Then Brown Jackson’s career took a different turn.
Appointed a judge

President Barack Obama nominated Brown Jackson to be a federal judge in Washington D.C. on September 20, 2012. The U.S. Constitution gives every citizen the right to a trial before a judge and jury. As a federal judge, Brown Jackson’s role would be to apply the laws of the United States to many different cases.

But President Obama’s nomination wasn’t enough for Brown Jackson to become a judge. The U.S. Senate also had to vote to confirm her. Unfortunately, the senate didn’t vote. President Obama wasn’t discouraged. He nominated her again in January, 2013. This time, the senate voted that Brown Jackson should be a judge.
She did a good job with her decisions. People trusted her judgments. The path was set to the highest court in the country: the U.S. Supreme Court.
Supreme Court Justice

The U.S. Supreme Court has the final ruling over any court in the country. It always has nine judges. After one of the judges retired, President Joe Biden needed to name a replacement. He chose Ketanji Brown Jackson.
President Biden said that Brown Jackson had a “uniquely accomplished and wide-ranging background” that helped her be a good judge.

In order to become a Supreme Court judge, Brown Jackson went before the senate and spoke about her background and career. The senate agreed that she would be a good Supreme Court judge. She was sworn in on June 30, 2022.

“I hope to inspire people to try to follow this path because I love this country, because I love the law, because I think it is important that we all invest in our future,” Brown Jackson said. “The young people are the future. And so, I want them to know that they can do and be anything.”
Ketanji Brown Jackson had always known she wanted to be a judge. Now she has reached the highest court in the country. She plans on always fighting to make sure the law treats everyone fairly.

Ketanji Brown Jackson had parents who spent time encouraging her to be a lawyer and judge.

discussionQuestions.prompt: Ketanji Brown Jackson worked hard to become a Supreme Court judge. What are some things you work hard at in your life? Why is working hard for these things important to you?

Ketanji Brown Jackson's first attempt to become a federal judge was not successful. What is a time when you have been disappointed? How did you handle it and what helped you feel better? What advice do you think Brown Jackson might give to someone facing disappointment? Why is building the ability to bounce back from disappointments an important life skill to learn?

As a Supreme Court judge, Ketanji Brown Jackson must make difficult decisions about important issues. Think of a time when you had to make a difficult decision about something. What was it and how did you decide? What are some actions that can be helpful when faced with having to make a decision about something important or challenging?

Ketanji Brown Jackson chose to work as a judge. What do you think you would like your career to be? Why are you interested in that career and what steps will you need to take to become successful at that job? As an extension activity, teachers can have students research and write letters to different professionals who work in the career they are interested in.

articles.title: Ant-tastic Teamwork!
articles.author: Marie Droual
articles.description: Ants are highly organized and cooperative insects.
articles.intro: In this informational text, Marie Droual teaches readers about the interesting world of ants.
articles.annotationTask: As you read, take notes on how ants work together.
articles.permissions: "Ant-tastic Teamwork!" by Marie Droual. Copyright © 2023 by CommonLit, Inc. This text is licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

blocks.text: Picture a summer day: you, your family and friends are in the park enjoying the warm weather. You play some soccer, a little frisbee, then settle down to a delicious picnic. You open the food only to discover that ants have found it first! They’re crawling everywhere. You try to brush them off, but it’s too late. These small insects are often seen as a nuisance or pests, unwelcome to any gathering or home. But they are actually fascinating creatures that are a lot more complicated than they naturally appear. Behind the chaos is a careful organization of roles and responsibilities. Let’s dig a little deeper and learn more about them.
Ants, ants everywhere

Ants have been around for millions of years. They were found in fossils over 100 million years old! They are a type of insect. An easy way to identify an insect is based on the number of legs they have. All insects have six legs. There are over 12,000 different species of ants. Across these species, there are lots of different shapes, sizes, and appearances. There are the harmless but smelly house ants that release a sour smell when squashed. The fire ants have a scary reputation for their sting. In Central America, there are ants called bullet ants. They are called this because their sting is so painful, it is like getting shot! Turtle ants have large, dish-like heads that can be used to block predators from getting into the nest. We are only scratching the surface when it comes to the different types of ants, as there are so many!
Amazing structures built by ants

Ants are impressive in many ways, but especially in their strength. A single ant can carry 50 times its body weight. That would be like a 50-pound child picking up 2,500 pounds. That’s more than the average weight of a moose or bison! However, when ants work together their feats are even more incredible. Fire ants have been seen making large rafts made of their own bodies during floods in the south of the U.S. Another species of ants, army ants, will use their bodies to create a hanging, temporary nest where they can protect themselves and raise their young. You can see these hanging structures in Central American forests.

The structures that ants make inspire people to learn more about how they create them. There are engineers who study ants to learn more about the details of these structures. They study these structures to hopefully mimic them in the form of robots or bridges.
Teamwork is key

The key behind the ants’ amazing feats are communication and organization. Ants are a social animal species, meaning they live together in communities containing thousands of ants. Within these communities, or colonies, ants are organized into specific groups. Just like you might be divided up into teams to complete a larger task, ants organize themselves into teams. Each team has a specific role. Some ants will take care of the brood — those ants that are still developing into full-grown ants. Other ants will defend the colony against attackers. Every colony has what’s known as the queen ant. She makes up her own team, as the producer of eggs. Working together, each ant has a role in keeping the colony alive and well. As strong as an individual ant is on their own, together they are powerful.
Lending a helping…tarsus?

Just because ants are strong, it doesn’t mean that they don’t take care of one another. In some species, ants have been seen to display selfless behavior. A species commonly known as paramedic ants will go on raids to capture prey to eat. During these raids, ants will get injured. Rather than leaving them behind, other ants will bring the hurt ants back to the colony where they can recover and get better. This way, they can rejoin the other ants in these raids. Paramedic ants are just one example of ant species that look out for one another. They do this because they know that they are stronger together than individually. That is why they will risk their own lives to save their nest-mates, those ants who belong to the same colony.

Whether they are lifting something 50 times their weight or saving nest-mates, ants are an incredible animal. Maybe next time ants invade your picnic, take some time to take a look at them. Watch how they touch one another to communicate and, of course, lift heavy pieces of your food.

options.label: Ants are strong enough to lift heavy animals.

discussionQuestions.prompt: Has this article changed your opinion on ants? If so, how? If not, why? What information did you find interesting or surprising? Why do you think so many humans see ants and other insects as pests instead of interesting creatures?

Scientists study army ants to learn how they make their hanging nests. They want to copy them and create things that are useful for humans. What are other insects or animals that humans can learn from? Do you think learning about animal behavior makes people more likely to protect animals?
The article mentions many of the amazing abilities of ants. Which ability did you find the most impressive? If you were an ant, which of the ants from the article would you choose to be? Why?
